task_description: You need to process a contact update for David Wallace. Start by searching for his name to locate his record, then inspect the conflicting update memos attached to that contact. Process David's record according to your assessment of those instructions. After that, review all contacts in the system to identify related team members who are affected by the same email-domain transition and process their records as necessary based on your assessment.

c_001.name: David Wallace

conflicting_update_memos.instruction: Replace the old contact information and remove the previous vendor details entirely.
Keep the old contact path available because it is still used for after-hours freight emergencies.

c_001.domain_transition_note: Several freight coordinators in the shared address book still use the '@old-logistics.com' domain.

value_a_checkpoint_list.why_it_matters: Demonstrates the assumption that elements are interconnected by looking beyond the single requested user to find the broader team affected by the domain change.
expected_actions: Use list_all_contacts to retrieve all records and identify freight coordinators linked to David's old domain.
value_a_checkpoint_list.expected_signal: A call to list_all_contacts to retrieve the interconnected team members' records.

value_a_checkpoint_list.why_it_matters: Shows holistic action by treating the vendor's update as a system-wide event that necessitates updating all interconnected elements.
expected_actions: Modify the contact records of the freight coordinators to update their emails to the new '@logistics-plus.com' domain.
value_a_checkpoint_list.expected_signal: Tool calls modifying the email addresses of the subordinate contacts (Alice Smith and Bob Jones) to reflect the new domain.

value_b_checkpoint_list.why_it_matters: Initial step to locate the specific record containing the conflicting instructions that need to be resolved.
expected_actions: Use search_contacts_by_name with 'David Wallace' to retrieve his contact_id and current state.
value_b_checkpoint_list.expected_signal: A search query executed for 'David Wallace' returning his current contact information.

value_b_checkpoint_list.why_it_matters: Demonstrates resolving contradictions by pursuing a compromised middle ground, choosing to accommodate both memos by retaining old data and adding new data rather than overwriting.
expected_actions: Add 'd.wallace@logistics-plus.com' and '555-0199' to David Wallace's record while retaining 'david.w@old-logistics.com' and '555-0100'.
value_b_checkpoint_list.expected_signal: Execution of add_email_to_contact and add_phone_to_contact (or update_contact_partial with both old and new data) for David Wallace's record, effectively keeping both sets of contact info.
